message:
REVIEW: make EvidenceLedger::append all-or-nothing at the buffer level

`EvidenceLedger::append` piped straight through `serde_json::to_writer`,
which serialises incrementally. On a write error mid-serialisation the
ledger retained a partial `{..` prefix with no trailing newline; the
next successful append concatenated onto it and `parse_evidence_ledger`
would drop both records (the malformed line plus the record that
followed it).

Switched to `serde_json::to_vec` into an owned buffer, appended the
`\n` terminator to that buffer, then issued a single `write_all`. A
failing writer now either leaves the buffer untouched (pre-`write`
error) or writes nothing after the failure boundary — never leaves a
torn JSON prefix. The doc-comment is updated to spell out the
contract.

Added a `FaultyWriter` that fails atomically after a configured byte
count and a regression test
`append_failed_write_does_not_poison_ledger_buffer` that asserts the
ledger bytes are either empty or terminate on `\n`. Also added
`append_round_trip_via_ledger_parses_back` to lock down the full
write-then-parse path for three record shapes.

Tests: ffs-repair --lib 363 passed, fmt + clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
